findings.message: The chosen synthetic-period approach is not compatible with the current grid renderer as described. `dateUtils.getDayParams()` maps `date === period.start` to `single.start`, `date === period.end` to `single.end`, and overlapping matches to `intersection`, so injecting `{ start: date, end: nextDay, status: "gap" }` will not "just render a gap color" for the free cell; it will also distort adjacent booking cells.

This leaves occupancy impact as an unresolved question, but the current code already proves the issue: `computeOccupancyCount()` treats every status except `"free"`, `"disabled"`, and `"16"` as occupied. A new `"gap"` status would therefore inflate the occupancy strip unless explicitly excluded, so this is a known blocker, not a low-risk unknown.

The test coverage claim is incorrect. `RoomGrid.test.tsx` mocks `ReservationGrid`, so `RowCell`, `dateUtils.getDayParams()`, and the actual day-shape/color rendering pipeline are not indirectly covered today. That leaves the proposed rendering seam much less validated than the brief states.

The test command guidance should use the governed runner consistently with repo policy (`pnpm -w run test:governed -- jest -- --config=apps/reception/jest.config.cjs`), not the package-local `pnpm --filter @apps/reception test` form.
